_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Pancreatic pseudocyst/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Enlargement of the cyst to more than 6 cm
Symptoms (epigastric pain, mass, nausea, vomiting, feeling of bloating, deep ache in the abdomen, jaundice, chest pain, anorexia, weight loss, ascites, gastrointestinal hemorrhage)
No response to conservative line of treatment
Pseudocyst is persistent over 6 weeks or causing symptoms
Pancreatic pseudocysts with associated major ductal disruption or a transected pancreas require operative therapy with cyst enterostomy
Pseudocyst in relation to head and body of pancreas (for cystogastrostomy)
Cyst size of at least 6 cm (for cystogastrostomy)
Surgery done after 6 weeks (for cystogastrostomy, for wall to become fibrous)

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management
White blood cell count, serum amylase reports

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment indication for procedure?
Did USG/CECT/MRI Abdomen confirm the diagnosis?
Was the radiographic imaging indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Radiographic imaging not indicative of surgery
Clinical notes missing detailed history, signs & symptoms, or planned line of treatment
USG/CECT/MRI Abdomen not confirming the diagnosis
White blood cell count or serum amylase reports missing at pre-authorization
Cyst size not documented or below 6 cm for drainage/surgery
Pseudocyst not persistent for over 6 weeks when surgery is indicated
Detailed ICPs missing daily vitals or treatment details at claim submission
Detailed procedure/operative notes not available at claim submission
Discharge summary missing or lacking follow-up advice at claim submission

clinical_key_pointers: A pancreatic pseudocyst is a collection of amylase-rich fluid around the pancreas as a result of acute or chronic pancreatitis bound to the pancreas by inflammatory tissue. Fluid collection in the first 4 weeks is an acute fluid collection. After 4 weeks, it becomes an pseudocyst. Fluid is enclosed by a wall of fibrous granulation tissue and is called a pseudocyst. Pancreatic pseudocysts are the most common cystic lesions of the pancreas; accounting for 75-80% of such masses.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Decide referral of cases to the appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment of patients under PMJAY
